full_response:
Let  

• T be an arbitrary maximum-weight spanning tree of G,  
• w* = min{ w(e) : e ∈ T } be the smallest edge–weight that appears in T, and  
• e* be an edge of T whose weight is exactly w*.  

We shall prove that every maximum spanning tree of G contains **some** edge of weight w*.  
(The proof is by contradiction and uses the standard exchange argument for spanning trees.)

--------------------------------------------------------------------
Proof (by contradiction).

Assume, for contradiction, that there exists a maximum spanning tree  
T′ that does **not** contain any edge of weight w*; i.e.

  ∀ e ∈ T′ w(e) > w*.                                    (1)

Step 1.  Disconnect T at e*.  
Removing e* breaks T into two connected components; denote their vertex
sets by A and B (so e* joins A and B in T).

Step 2.  Locate an A–B edge in T′ that is **not** in T.  
Because T′ spans all vertices, there is a unique path in T′ from a vertex
of A to a vertex of B; let f be the first edge of this path that crosses
from A to B.  Then

 • f connects A and B,  
 • f ∉ T  (otherwise T\{e*} would already contain an A–B edge, contradicting
   the fact that e* is the only such edge in T), and  
 • by (1) w(f) > w*.                                     (2)

Step 3.  Exchange e* and f.  
Define  

  T″ = T − {e*} ∪ {f}.  

T″ is connected (f reconnects A and B) and has exactly |V|−1 edges, so it
is a spanning tree.

Step 4.  Compare the weights of T″ and T.  
Using (2),

  w(T″) = w(T) − w(e*) + w(f) > w(T)   because w(f) > w(e*) = w*.  

Thus w(T″) > w(T), contradicting the assumption that T is already a
maximum-weight spanning tree.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
The contradiction arose from assuming that a maximum spanning tree T′
could avoid all edges of weight w*.  Hence every maximum spanning tree
must in fact contain (at least) one edge whose weight equals w*.

∎

Key insight:  
Take the minimum-weight edge e* of one maximum spanning tree T.  Any other
maximum spanning tree that omits all edges of that weight can be improved
by replacing e* with a heavier edge that crosses the cut defined by e*,
contradicting maximality.

\boxed{\text{All maximum spanning trees of }G\text{ contain an edge of weight }w^*.}  

Confidence: 82%
extracted_answer: \text{All maximum spanning trees of }G\text{ contain an edge of weight }w^*.
